fresh_facts.claim: The U.S. planned to deploy 3,000-4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East, building on prior reinforcements and raising U.S. troop levels beyond 50,000 in the region.
Approximately 2,500 Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit aboard USS Tripoli were ordered toward the Strait of Hormuz on March 14 amid Iranian attacks on shipping.
U.S. and Israeli strikes have hit 9,000 Iranian targets since February 28, 2026.
Iran escalated missile and drone attacks on U.S. and Israeli military sites and commercial shipping near the Strait of Hormuz, which handles 20% of global oil.
As of March 24, 2026, 13 U.S. troops were killed and 290 wounded (10 seriously) in the conflict with Iran.
Iran rejected a U.S. 15-point ceasefire proposal from March 25, demanding a halt to strikes on its officials, war reparations, no future wars, and retained control of the Strait of Hormuz.
President Trump threatened to 'unleash hell' if no ceasefire deal with Iran is reached.
Thailand secured an agreement with Iran on March 28, 2026 for safe passage of its oil vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council on March 28, 2026, shifting decision-making to military-security structures.
Mojtaba Khamenei was named Supreme Leader of Iran on March 12, 2026, following the killing of the prior Supreme Leader.
IRGC hardliners from the Iran-Iraq war era filled leadership voids following the death of Khamenei, sidelining political figures and insisting on U.S. concessions.
Iran enforced one of its longest nationwide internet blackouts amid U.S. and Israeli strikes as of March 28, 2026.
A jailbreak occurred in Mariwan, a Kurdish city, after a detention facility was struck by U.S. or Israeli forces.
Oil prices remained elevated due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions caused by Iranian attacks on shipping.
Public U.S. approval for military strikes against Iran was at 35% as of March 24, 2026.
Europe warned against promoting a preferred Iranian leader, citing risks to democratic principles as of March 28, 2026.
U.S. CENTCOM reported significant Iranian military capability degradation from U.S. and Israeli strikes.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces escalating U.S. and Israeli military pressure with over 9,000 targets struck since late February, while the new Supreme Leader and military-dominated security council reject ceasefire terms and continue attacks on shipping. Internal control mechanisms are being tested by internet blackouts and detention facility breaches amid succession uncertainty.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mojtaba Khamenei appointed as Supreme Leader on March 12, 2026, followed by military hardliners consolidating control through security council appointments on March 28, 2026.
